seoTitle: Lake George vs Lake Haven — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lake George and Lake Haven. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lake George vs Lake Haven: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake George and Lake Haven.
heading: Lake George vs Lake Haven — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake George, New South Wales and Lake Haven, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Haven has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Lake George (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake George or Lake Haven safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake George has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Lake Haven scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake George scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Haven scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake George or Lake Haven?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Haven has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
